message.content:
Adversarially verify this reverse-engineered architecture claim. Default to UNSUPPORTED if you cannot
confirm it from a PRIMARY source (paper, repo, official doc) or from the artifact text itself.

SYSTEM: dalpha-deepresearch (Dalpha / 달파, Seoul-based AI agent startup; agent framework reportedly named "Cobra"). GPT-5.5 board: 53.10 overall, rank 5.
HAS PAPER: false
ARCHITECTURE CLAIMED: PUBLISHED RECORD: essentially nothing. Searched arXiv, GitHub, HF, company site, and Korean-language press (달파, 딥리서치, 에이전트). There is NO paper, NO code, NO tech report, NO model/dataset card for dalpha-deepresearch. What exists is press: (a) TheElec (thelec.net/news/articleView.html?idxno=11532) — Dalpha claims #1 on GIFT-Eval, DeepResearch Bench and DRB-II with "a proprietary general-purpose agent framework" not task-specialized; no scores, no architecture, no third-party verification. (b) ETNews 2026-05-07 (etnews.com/20260507000224) — framework named "Cobra"; scored 79.11% on OpenAI MLE-Bench (vs Google CAIR MARS+ 62.67, Baidu FM-Agent 64.44); described as using a "world model methodology" that internally simulates environment-model interaction to predict future outcomes, enabling autonomous exploration and self-improvement; company says a paper is PLANNED but not published. Treat "world model" as marketing until a paper lands. So the architecture below is reverse-engineered from the 100 artifacts, not from any disclosure.

RETRIEVAL (inferred): deliberately SMALL and primary-source-biased. Median 30 unique URLs per report (range 7-92) vs gemini-2.5-pro 106, bodhi 60, openai-DR 41. Sources are official docs, GitHub repos, standards (GB/T), ministry statistics, journal DOIs — not blogspam. On the lit-review task (id 72) every one of 35 refs is a journal article with a DOI and zero web URLs; on the Obsidian task (id 66) sources are obsidian.md/help pages, plugin listings and repo manifest.json files. So retrieval is TARGETED AT AUTHORITATIVE ARTIFACTS and the source TYPE is selected by task genre (a genre-conditioned retrieval policy). Median 69 inline [n] markers over ~30 sources = each source is re-cited ~2.3x; citation density 15 markers/1k words, ~1 per 67 words.

OUTLINE: bespoke, not templated. Heading uniqueness across all 100 reports = 0.896 (2993 unique of 3339 headings). The only invariants are: H1 title (100%), an executive-summary-type opening section (78/100: "Executive summary"/"执行摘要"/"核心结论"/"结论摘要"/"Short answer"/"Bottom line"), a Conclusion (~65/100), and "## Sources" as the last section (100/100). Everything between is derived from the question. It writes ANSWER-SHAPED outlines, not TOPIC-SHAPED ones (e.g. id 68 K8s: "Why standard Cluster Autoscaler may not be enough" -> "Strategy taxonomy" -> "Patterns A-F" -> "Provider-specific" -> "Recommended architectures IF demand is calendar-predictable / IF event-driven / IF latency-critical").

LENGTH: the shortest report on the board by 2-4x. Median 5,224 words (EN 3,956 / CN 6,290) vs cellcog-max 20,957, gemini 16,144, lunon 14,998, WhaleCloud 10,296, bodhi 9,883. It beats gemini (49.98) by 3.1 pts with 1/3 the words and 1/4 the sources. corr(words, insight) within dalpha = 0.21 — length is nearly irrelevant to its own scores.

MICRO-FORM: median paragraph 37 words (short, one-claim paragraphs). Bullets are SHORT enumerations (median 9 words; only 20% bold-led) — unlike gemini (56-word bullets = bullet-as-paragraph) or cellcog (23 words, 55% bold-led). Argument lives in prose and tables; bullets only enumerate. 83/100 use tables, 91/100 use --- rules between sections. Reads as high-signal-per-word, which is why readability = 53.20, the HIGHEST of any system on the board.
COMPOSITION METHOD CLAIMED: THE KEY MECHANISM — "the inference lives in the SCHEMA, not in the sentences."

dalpha does NOT get insight by writing speculative sentences. Almost every declarative sentence is still tightly attached to a citation (15 markers/1k words). What it does instead is INVENT AN ORGANIZING FRAME that no source contains, and then populate that frame with cited facts. The novel/analytic content is carried by structure, not by unverifiable prose. Four concrete frame types, all observed:

1. SCOPE CONTRACT (口径 / "What X means"). ~31/100 open with a definitional section that states what counts and what doesn't, usually as an inclusion/exclusion table. id 44 opens with a table of five scope variants and marks each "included in main estimate / excluded, reported separately". id 66 opens with "What 'Notion-like multi-view database' means" and derives 4 required capabilities BEFORE naming any candidate. This is a judgment (it is an authorial decision) that reads as rigor, and it makes every later claim commensurable. It is the single cheapest insight+instruction-following lever on the board.

2. TRANSPARENT ARITHMETIC MODEL (the Fermi engine). This is how it produces numbers that exist in NO source without hallucinating. id 44 (annual demand for metro pantograph carbon strips, insight 0.570): (a) fleet denominator from official statistics [1][2][3]; (b) a PARAMETER TABLE where every row is (parameter | estimated value | BASIS — with citation or "this is an estimation convention, not official statistics"); (c) a LOW/MID/HIGH SCENARIO TABLE producing 24k-57k units; (d) plus new-build fitment, giving "30k-60k/yr, median judgment ~40k/yr"; (e) an INDEPENDENT CROSS-CHECK against a real procurement tender (860 units / RMB 1.4448M -> RMB 1,680/unit) and an explicit reconciliation of why the check disagrees; (f) a monetary estimate = quantity x unit price. Every INPUT is span-verifiable. The COMBINATION step is explicitly labeled as authorial estimate. That is cross-source inference done in a form that survives a fact-checker.

3. EVIDENCE-STATUS LEDGER (turning non-findings into insight). 40/100 contain an explicit "the evidence cannot establish X" block, and crucially it is placed BEFORE the weak numbers, not buried. id 44 §3.1: "public tender share != real market share" with four enumerated reasons, then presents share tables labeled "Sample A" / "Sample B" with the sample total (RMB 7.961M) and refuses to generalize. id 66's comparison matrix contains cells reading "No documented distinct List view", "Not verified", "no separate Calendar verified" — and it then DERIVES a conclusion from the absence ("therefore not a full four-view replacement"). Absence of evidence is converted into an analytic distinction instead of an omission. This is why an extraction-grounded system does not have to lie to gain insight: "not documented" is itself a verifiable, load-bearing claim.

4. VERDICT + ROUTER LAYER (commitment). It commits to an answer. Per-item "### Verdict" blocks (id 66: every candidate gets Strengths / Weaknesses / Verdict), a global "## Bottom line" / "最终判断" / "结论先说" / "Short answer", and a CONDITIONAL ROUTER: 48/100 contain "Best for X -> use Y" / "If demand is calendar-predictable -> ..." / "如果你...". This is a decision function over the evidence, which no single source supplies. Note it wins on the RANKING itself: the id 66 exec summary is a ranked 1-5 shortlist with one-line reasons, produced before any evidence is shown.

PROCESS SHAPE (inferred, medium confidence): the invariant skeleton (title -> exec summary -> bespoke body -> conclusion -> Sources) plus near-total heading uniqueness plus the fact that the exec summary is a ranked/quantified ANSWER that presupposes the whole body implies a plan-then-write pipeline where the OUTLINE IS SYNTHESIZED FIRST FROM AN EVIDENCE LEDGER, then sections are written against it, then the exec summary is written LAST (or rewritten last) from the finished verdicts. Evidence for a late/global pass: exec summary numbers, the mid-report scenario table and the conclusion agree exactly in id 44; the id 66 exec summary ranking is identical to the Bottom-line ranking 4,000 words later. Evidence AGAINST a heavy multi-agent parallel-section writer: no section-boundary repetition, no redundant re-introductions, uniform voice, no per-section source silos (the same [n] indices recur across distant sections, so the bibliography is GLOBAL and fixed before writing, not per-section). I found NO direct evidence of an adversarial critic pass; the "not verified / not documented" language is better explained by a verification pass over CLAIMS (each cell/parameter checked against a source, with the check's outcome written into the text) than by a prose critic.

WHAT IT DOES NOT DO: it does not pad. It does not template. It does not bury argument in bullets. It does not maximize sources. It is last among the top-5 on comprehensiveness (52.58) and first on the whole board on readability (53.20) — it deliberately trades breadth for tightness and still ranks 5th.
EVIDENCE CITED: Artifacts (all measured over all 100 tasks, not just 72):
- /home/polaris/polaris_project/drb_corpus/gpt55_board/dalpha-deepresearch.jsonl (100 reports)
- /home/polaris/polaris_project/drb_corpus/gpt55_board/scores/dalpha-deepresearch/race_result.txt -> Comprehensiveness 0.5258 | Insight 0.5294 | Instruction Following 0.5387 | Readability 0.5320 | Overall 0.5310
- /home/polaris/polaris_project/drb_corpus/gpt55_board/scores/dalpha-deepresearch/raw_results.jsonl (per-task dims)
- /home/polaris/polaris_project/drb_corpus/gpt55_board/leaderboard_gpt55.csv

Measured (my scripts over the jsonl):
- median words: dalpha 5,224 | cellcog-max 20,957 | gemini-2.5-pro 16,144 | lunon 14,998 | WhaleCloud 10,296 | bodhi 9,883 | openai-DR 11,828
- median unique URLs cited: dalpha 30 | gemini 106 | bodhi 60 | openai-DR 41 | lunon 12
- inline [n] markers: dalpha median 69/report, 15.1 per 1k words
- heading uniqueness (unique/total H1-H3 across 100): dalpha 0.896; "## Sources" is the last section in 100/100; exec-summary-type opening in 78/100
- median paragraph 37 words; median bullet 9 words (gemini 56, cellcog 23)
- 83/100 tables, 91/100 horizontal rules, 40/100 explicit evidence-gap statements, 43/100 explicit labeled assumptions, 48/100 conditional recommendation routers, 31/100 scope/method sections
- corr(words, insight) within dalpha = 0.21; corr(words, overall) = 0.27

Key exemplar reports (ids in that jsonl): 44 (Fermi model + scenario table + cross-check + share-sample ledger; insight 0.570), 66 (scope contract + comparison matrix with "not verified" cells + per-option Verdict + Bottom line), 68 (invented pattern taxonomy A-F + conditional router), 72 (journal-only lit review, 35 DOI refs, zero web URLs), 1 (CN, bespoke 9-tier framework the prompt's premise did not supply).

Published record:
- https://www.thelec.net/news/articleView.html?idxno=11532 — Dalpha claims #1 on GIFT-Eval / DeepResearch Bench / DRB-II; NO scores, NO architecture. Company announcement, unverified.
- https://www.etnews.com/20260507000224 — "Cobra" framework; MLE-Bench 79.11%; "world model" simulation of environment-model interaction; paper PLANNED, not published.
- No arXiv paper, no GitHub repo, no HF card, no tech report found for dalpha/Cobra. Searched EN + KO (달파, 코브라 에이전트, 딥리서치) + CN terms.

Check: does the cited source actually say this? Is it current (2026)? Is an "architecture" being inferred from
output text and then stated as fact? Inference from artifacts is LEGITIMATE but must be LABELLED as inference,
not presented as documented design. Flag any place that line is crossed.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- dataviz: Use this skill whenever you are about to create ANY chart, graph, plot, dashboard, or data visualization, in ANY output medium — an HTML or React artifact, inline SVG, plotting code in any library (matplotlib, plotly, d3, Recharts, …), an image/PNG you will render and upload, or a chart shared into Slack. Read it BEFORE writing the first line of chart code, choosing chart colors, building a stat tile / meter / KPI row, or laying out a dashboard. Produces visualizations that read as one system — elegant, accessible, consistent in light and dark — using a brand-neutral placeholder palette you swap for your own. Teaches a design-system-agnostic method: a form heuristic, a color formula with a runnable validator, mark specs, and interaction rules. A validated default palette is documented in `references/palette.md` — swap that file's values for your brand's. Triggers on: "chart", "graph", "plot", "data viz", "visualization", "dashboard", "analytics", "visualize data", "categorical colors", "sequential / diverging palette", "stat tile", "sparkline", "heatmap", "legend", "axis", "tooltip", "chart colors", "color by series".
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by exercising it end-to-end and observing behavior — drive the affected flow, not just tests or typecheck. Run before committing nontrivial changes; bootstraps this repo's project verify skill if none exists yet. Don't invoke it on a diff that only touches tests, docs, or other code with no runtime surface to drive (a change to product source always has one) — there's nothing to observe.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.text: I'll verify this against the actual artifacts.

input.command:
cd /home/polaris/polaris_project/drb_corpus/gpt55_board && python3 -c "
import json,re,statistics as st
from collections import Counter
def load(f): return [json.loads(l) for l in open(f) if l.strip()]
def art(r):
    for k in ('article','answer','response','content','report','text'):
        if isinstance(r.get(k),str): return r[k]
    return ''
def words(t):
    cn=len(re.findall(r'[一-鿿]',t))
    en=len(re.findall(r'[A-Za-z0-9][A-Za-z0-9\'\-]*',t))
    return cn+en
files=['dalpha-deepresearch.jsonl','cellcog-max.jsonl','gemini-2.5-pro-deepresearch.jsonl','lunon_full100_FINAL.submission.jsonl','WhaleCloud-DocChain_0612.jsonl','bodhi.jsonl','openai-deepresearch.jsonl']
for f in files:
    rs=load(f); ws=[words(art(r)) for r in rs]
    urls=[len(set(re.findall(r'https?://[^\s\)\]\>\"]+',art(r)))) for r in rs]
    print(f'{f:45s} n={len(rs):3d} medwords={int(st.median(ws)):6d} medURLs={int(st.median(urls)):4d}')
"

content.text: Word counts and URL counts line up (my tokenizer differs slightly on CJK). Now the structural claims.

input.command:
cd /home/polaris/polaris_project/drb_corpus/gpt55_board && python3 -c "
import json,re,statistics as st
from collections import Counter
def load(f): return [json.loads(l) for l in open(f) if l.strip()]
d=load('dalpha-deepresearch.jsonl')
def W(t):
    return len(re.findall(r'[一-鿿]',t))+len(re.findall(r\"[A-Za-z0-9][A-Za-z0-9'\-]*\",t))
H=[];tot=0;last_sources=0;execop=0;tables=0;rules=0
marks=[];dens=[];paras=[];bullets=[];boldled=0;bul_tot=0
for r in d:
    t=r['article']
    hs=re.findall(r'^(#{1,3})\s+(.+)$',t,re.M)
    names=[h[1].strip() for h in hs]
    H+= [n.lower() for n in names]; tot+=len(names)
    if names and re.search(r'sources|参考|来源|references|参考文献',names[-1],re.I): last_sources+=1
    # first h2-ish section after title
    body=[n for n in names[1:]] 
    if body and re.search(r'exec|summary|执行摘要|核心结论|结论摘要|short answer|bottom line|摘要|结论先说|一句话|TL;DR',body[0],re.I): execop+=1
    if '|' in t and re.search(r'^\|.*\|',t,re.M): tables+=1
    if re.search(r'^\s*(---|\*\*\*|___)\s*$',t,re.M): rules+=1
    m=len(re.findall(r'\[\d+\]',t)); marks.append(m); dens.append(m/max(W(t),1)*1000)
    for p in t.split('\n'):
        p=p.strip()
        if not p or p.startswith(('#','|','>','---')): continue
        if re.match(r'^([-*+]|\d+\.)\s',p):
            b=re.sub(r'^([-*+]|\d+\.)\s','',p); bullets.append(W(b)); bul_tot+=1
            if b.startswith('**'): boldled+=1
        else: paras.append(W(p))
uniq=len(set(H))
print('headings total',tot,'unique',uniq,'uniqueness %.3f'%(uniq/tot))
print('Sources-last (loose):',last_sources,'/100')
print('exec-summary-type opening:',execop,'/100')
print('tables',tables,'/100  hrules',rules,'/100')
print('median [n] markers/report',st.median(marks),' median density/1k',round(st.median(dens),1))
print('median paragraph words',st.median(paras),' median bullet words',st.median(bullets),' bold-led %',round(100*boldled/max(bul_tot,1),1))
"

content.text: Directionally right, exact micro-numbers a bit off. Now the composition-method counts and the exemplar reports.

input.command:
cd /home/polaris/polaris_project/drb_corpus/gpt55_board && python3 -c "
import json,re
d={json.loads(l)['id']:json.loads(l)['article'] for l in open('dalpha-deepresearch.jsonl')}
pats={
 'evidence-gap':r'(cannot (be )?(establish|verif|confirm)|not verified|no documented|not documented|无法(证实|核实|确认)|未(见|能)(公开|证实|查到)|证据不足|无公开(数据|资料)|not publicly (available|documented))',
 'labeled-assumption':r'(assumption|assumed|estimation convention|estimate[sd]? (basis|convention)|作者估计|估算(口径|约定|假设)|假设|本文假设|非官方统计)',
 'router':r'(best for|if (you|your|demand|the)[^\n]{0,60}(->|→|then|use|choose)|如果你|若你|适合(场景|人群)|推荐(场景|选择)：|建议选择)',
 'scope':r'(^#{2,3}[^\n]*(口径|scope|what [\x27\"“]?.{0,40} means|定义|范围界定|术语|界定|method)|included in main estimate|inclusion|exclusion)',
 'verdict':r'(###\s*(verdict|结论|判定)|bottom line|最终判断|结论先说|short answer)',
}
for k,p in pats.items():
    c=sum(1 for t in d.values() if re.search(p,t,re.I|re.M))
    print(f'{k:20s} {c}/100')
print()
for i in [44,66,68,72,1]:
    t=d.get(i) or d.get(str(i),'')
    hs=re.findall(r'^#{1,3}\s+(.+)$',t,re.M)
    print('=== id',i,'| words~',len(t.split()),'| headings:',len(hs))
    print('  ',' || '.join(hs[:9]))
"

import json,re,statistics as st

"

cd /home/polaris/polaris_project/drb_corpus/gpt55_board && python3 -c "
import json,re,statistics as st
def W(t): return len(re.findall(r'[一-鿿]',t))+len(re.findall(r\"[A-Za-z0-9][A-Za-z0-9'\-]*\",t))
arts={json.loads(l)['id']:json.loads(l)['article'] for l in open('dalpha-deepresearch.jsonl')}
rr=[json.loads(l) for l in open('scores/dalpha-deepresearch/raw_results.jsonl')]
import math
def corr(a,b):
    n=len(a);ma=sum(a)/n;mb=sum(b)/n
    num=sum((x-ma)*(y-mb) for x,y in zip(a,b))
    den=math.sqrt(sum((x-ma)**2 for x in a)*sum((y-mb)**2 for y in b))
    return num/den
w=[];ins=[];ov=[]
for r in rr:
    t=arts.get(r['id'])
    if t is None: continue
    w.append(W(t)); ins.append(r['insight'])
    ov.append(r.get('overall_score') or r.get('overall') or sum(r[k] for k in ('comprehensiveness','insight','instruction_following','readability'))/4)
print('n',len(w))
print('corr(words,insight)  = %.3f'%corr(w,ins))
print('corr(words,overall)  = %.3f'%corr(w,ov))
print('id44 insight', [r['insight'] for r in rr if r['id']==44])
print('EN/CN median words:')
en=[W(t) for i,t in arts.items() if len(re.findall(r'[一-鿿]',t))<200]
cn=[W(t) for i,t in arts.items() if len(re.findall(r'[一-鿿]',t))>=200]
print(' EN n=%d med=%d | CN n=%d med=%d'%(len(en),st.median(en),len(cn),st.median(cn)))
"

用公式估算：

> **年度需求 = 接触网受电列车数 × 单列年需求**

| 高情景 | 9,500列 | 5—6根/列年 | 约4.8万—5.7万根 |

再加上新车初装。2'

content.content:
10][11]。不同城市、型号、是否含托架/组件/ADD气道、运输税费和服务会造成较大差异。

若按**1,600—2,500元/根**估算：

> **国内地铁/城轨受电弓碳滑板年度市场金额约0.5亿—1.5亿元。**

这只是碳滑板本体和相近采购口径的粗估；若采购标的包含受电弓组件、ADD、检测服务、框架备件包，金额会显著高于单根滑板口径。

---

## 3. 供应商格局与份额

### 3.1 先说限制：公开份额不等于真实市场份额

目前公开资料不足以得出“全国真实市场份额”。原因有四个：

1. 很多碳滑板随新车、受电弓总成或备件包采购，不单独披露。
2. 年度采购公告常有金额无数量，或有数量无金额。
3. 中标主体可能是制造商，也可能是代理商、授权供货商、车辆/备件集成商。
4. 不同城市使用原厂件、试用准入件、单一来源件、框架备件，公开可见度差异很大。

因此，以下份额只能称为：

> **公开招投标样本中的合同主体金额份额。**

### 3.2 公开样本金额份额

基于已披露金额、项目名称明确为碳滑板/滑板条/组件的样本，主要可计算的是北京万高众业与石家庄中车轨道交通装备。

| 样本口径 | 北京万高众业 | 石家庄中车轨道交通装备 | 说明 |
|---|---:|---:|---|
| 样本A：已披露历史合同金额样本 | 约81.9% | 约18.1% | 样本总额约796.1万元；北京万高覆盖济南、成都、天津、石家庄、北京等合同线索；石家庄中车为石家庄项目[10][12] |
| 样本B：样本A + 石家庄2025—2026年3号线第一候选项目 | 约67.6% | 约32.4% | 将石家庄中车168万元第一候选报价计入后得到；仍非最终全国份额[10] |

这个样本过小，且石家庄中车是否为实际碳滑板制造商尚未穿透，因此不能把上述比例当作全国市场份额。

### 3.3 主要供应商定位

| 企业/品牌 | 角色判断 | 证据与备注 |
|---|---|---|
| **北京万高众业科技股份有限公司** | 国内重要碳滑板制造商/直接供货商 | 公开样本金额最高；永贵电器公告称其主要经营轨道交通受电弓碳滑板等业务[18]；CRCC公告出现其碳滑板认证信息[17] |
| **苏州东南佳新材料股份有限公司** | 国内重要制造商 | 上海地铁单一来源公示明确其为碳滑板产品生产厂商[15]；公开转让说明书披露其主营轨道交通受电弓碳滑板、滑板碳条研发生产销售[19] |
| **石家庄中车轨道交通装备有限公司** | 公开样本中重要合同主体；制造商属性需穿透 | 石家庄项目中标/候选金额较高[10]；但公告允许制造商或授权代理商投标，不能直接判定其为碳滑板实际制造商 |
| **大同新成新材料/新成新材** | 国内制造商/材料商，城轨份额待核 | 年报披露受电弓碳滑板、CRCC、天津地铁等考核线索[20]；但本轮未核到高质量城轨中标金额样本 |
| **摩根系 Morgan/摩根新材料上海** | 外资/外资本土化重要品牌 | 摩根上海工厂资料显示其生产轨道交通碳滑板、集电靴等[22]；在南京、郑州等采购线索中出现 |
| **Mersen 美尔森、Schunk** | 国际受流材料/系统供应商 | 美尔森产品覆盖受电弓碳滑板等轨交受流件[23]；Schunk更多还涉及受电弓系统和碳材料，需区分总成商与滑板制造商 |
| **宜兴溢洋墨根、辽宁红德、邢台瑞普等** | 技术/认证/标准或候选供应商线索 | 在标准、认证、专利或行业资料中出现；城轨地铁公开中标份额需进一步核验 |

### 3.4 竞争格局的真实含义

目前更合理的判断不是“某一家占全国多少”，而是：

- **北京万高众业、苏州东南佳**是公开证据

content.content:
=== EXEC SUMMARY ===
Executive summary

If “replicate Notion databases” means **multiple saved views over the same underlying records**—Table, Kanban/Board, Calendar, and List, with filters, sorts, grouping, properties, and editable records—then the strongest Obsidian options are:

1. **Make.md** — best single-plugin, broad “Notion-like workspace/database” option. It documents list, table, calendar, and kanban/board context views over notes, plus properties and relational features. Its biggest weakness is complexity and less-certain view-by-view inline writeback documentation.  
2. **Obsidian Bases + Kanban/Board plugin + Calendar Bases** — best local-first, Obsidian-native stack. Native Bases gives Table/List; community Bases extensions add Kanban and Calendar. Strong for Markdown/YAML portability, weaker for all-in-one polish and maturity.  
3. **Database Views** by ekmungi — very Notion-like for Table, Ka

=== BOTTOM LINE ===
][47][48].

---

## Bottom line

For most users, the practical shortlist is:

1. **Make.md** — best single-plugin Notion-like database/workspace replacement.  
2. **Obsidian Bases + Kanban Bases View/Base Board + Calendar Bases** — best local-first, Obsidian-native, Markdown-backed database stack.  
3. **Database Views** — strong Table/Kanban/Calendar database mechanics, but lacks a documented List view.  
4. **TaskNotes** — best if your databases are mainly tasks.  
5. **Note Database** — good Table/Board/List option, but missing Calendar.

No Obsidian plugin perfectly duplicates Notion’s database system. The best choice depends on whether you value **all-in-one polish** (Make.md), **Markdown portability and o

content.text: id 66 fully confirmed — identical 1-5 ranking in exec summary and Bottom line 4,000 words later. Now the published-record claims (the two press URLs).

input.prompt: Does this article discuss Dalpha (달파) and an agent framework named Cobra? What benchmark scores (MLE-Bench) are given? Does it mention a "world model" methodology? Does it say a paper is planned/not yet published? Quote key sentences.
Does this article discuss Dalpha (달파) claiming #1 on GIFT-Eval, DeepResearch Bench, DRB-II? Does it give any scores or architecture details? Quote key sentences.

content.content:
# Article Analysis: Dalpha and Cobra Agent Framework

**Yes, the article discusses both Dalpha and their Cobra agent framework.**

## MLE-Bench Scores

Dalpha's Cobra Agent achieved a composite score of **79.11%**, surpassing competitors:
- Google AI Research (CAIR MARS+): 62.67%
- Baidu (FM-Agent): 64.44%

The article notes that "일부 과제에서는 AI가 인간 참가자 1위의 성과를 넘어서기도 했다" (in some tasks, the AI exceeded top human participant performance).

## World Model Methodology

Yes, the article confirms use of this approach: "환경과 모델의 상호작용을 내부적으로 시뮬레이션하며 미래 결과를 예측하는 월드 모델 방법론이 활용됐다" (a world model methodology simulating internal environment-model interactions to predict future outcomes was utilized).

## Publication Status

**Paper not yet published**: "달파는 이번 기술적 성과와 구체적인 구현 방식을 담은 연구 논문을 조만간 발표할 예정이다" (Dalpha plans to publish research detailing technical achievements and implementation methods soon).

# Article Analysis

**Yes**, the article discusses Dalpha's benchmark claims, but it provides **no numerical scores or architecture details**.

## Key Claims

The article states that Dalpha "ranked first in several major global AI benchmarks," specifically naming:
- Salesforce's GIFT-Eval
- Deep Research Bench
- Deep Research Bench II

## Relevant Quote

"The benchmarks in which Dalpha claimed the top position include Salesforce's GIFT-Eval, Deep Research Bench and Deep Research Bench II, a more rigorous evaluation framework."

## What's Missing

The article emphasizes that their "proprietary general-purpose agent framework delivered leading performance across multiple domains" but does **not provide**:
- Specific benchmark scores or percentages
- Technical architecture details
- Comparisons with competing systems' scores

The focus is instead on the company's team composition and their next project (a "Social World Model").

content.text: Both press URLs check out exactly as described, including "paper planned, not yet published." Now the negative claim: no paper/repo/card.

Based on the web search results, I found several relevant resources related to DeepResearch agent architectures and benchmarks, though I didn't find a specific project called "dalpha-deepresearch." Here's what I found:

## DeepResearch Benchmark and Agents

DeepResearch Bench comprises 100 PhD-level tasks across 22 fields, using the RACE (Reference-based Adaptive Criteria-driven Evaluation) framework.

### Key Agent Architectures:

1. **Deep Researcher (Sequential Plan Refinement)**: Deep Researcher is an advanced autonomous agent architecture designed to generate detailed, PhD-level research reports on complex topics, utilizing Sequential Research Plan Refinement and Candidates Crossover to achieve superior depth and coherence.

2. **DeepResearchAgent (SkyworkAI)**: DeepResearchAgent is a hierarchical multi-agent system designed for deep research tasks and general-purpose task solving, leveraging a top-level planning agent to coordinate multiple specialized lower-level agents.

3. **DeepResearch Bench II**: DeepResearch Bench II (DRB2) is the follow-up to DeepResearch Bench, with a stronger focus on measuring the gap between deep research systems and human experts by decomposing expert-written reports into hierarchical rubrics covering presentation, analysis, and evidence.

## GitHub Resources

The relevant repositories include:
- https://github.com/SauravP97/deep-researcher-reflect-evolve
- https://github.com/SkyworkAI/DeepResearchAgent
- https://github.com/Ayanami0730/deep_research_bench (Official DeepResearch Bench)
- https://github.com/imlrz/DeepResearch-Bench-II

If you're looking for a specific "Dalpha" project, it may have a different name or might be less widely documented. Could you provide additional context about this project?

content.text: I have everything I need. Let me do one final check on the process-shape evidence (global bibliography, exec/conclusion agreement in id 44).

input.command:
cd /home/polaris/polaris_project/drb_corpus/gpt55_board && python3 -c "
import json,re
d={json.loads(l)['id']:json.loads(l)['article'] for l in open('dalpha-deepresearch.jsonl')}
t=d[44]
# exec summary numbers vs body vs conclusion
print('=== 结论摘要 ==='); i=t.find('结论摘要'); print(t[i:i+380])
# global bibliography test: do same [n] recur across distant sections?
secs=re.split(r'\n## ',t)
print()
print('--- citation index reuse across sections (id44) ---')
sets=[set(re.findall(r'\[(\d+)\]',s)) for s in secs]
for a in range(len(sets)):
    for b in range(a+2,len(sets)):
        ov=sets[a]&sets[b]
        if ov: print(f'  sec{a} & sec{b} share {sorted(ov)}')
" | head -30

content.content:
=== 结论摘要 ===
结论摘要

1. **没有公开的全国官方统计能直接回答“地铁碳滑板一年用多少根”。** 可用的做法是用车辆保有量、接触网受流比例、受电弓配置、滑板寿命和采购公告样本进行估算。
2. 按**中国内地、以地铁/城轨受电弓碳滑板为主口径**，2024—2025年前后的年需求量可估为：  
   **约3万—6万根/年，中位判断约4万根/年左右。**  
   其中，**存量运营替换是绝对主体**，新车初装约占小头，粗略为**2,000—4,000根/年**。
3. 若把第三轨/接触轨线路的**集电靴、受流器滑板**也放进“城市轨道交通车辆用炭滑板”广义口径，需求会增加，但这已不是狭义“受电弓碳滑板”，公开数据不足以可靠合并。
4. **供应商真实全国份额无法从公开资料可靠计算。** 公开招投标样本显示，北京万高众业、苏州东南佳、石家庄中车轨道交通装备、摩根

--- citation index reuse across sections (id44) ---
  sec2 & sec5 share ['4', '5']
  sec2 & sec7 share ['4', '5']
  sec3 & sec5 share ['3', '7']
  sec3 & sec7 share ['1', '10', '11', '2', '3', '6', '7', '8']
  sec4 & sec7 share ['10', '12', '15', '17', '18', '19', '20', '22', '23', '24']
  sec5 & sec7 share ['17', '19', '20', '22', '25', '3', '4', '5', '7']

content.text: Everything checks out. Writing up the verdict.

input.reasoning:
I verified this against primary artifacts (the corpus + score files) and the two primary press sources, and tried to break it. It survives almost everything.

EPISTEMIC DISCIPLINE (the thing you asked me to police) — PASSES. The claim does NOT present an inferred architecture as documented design. It explicitly states "the architecture below is reverse-engineered from the 100 artifacts, not from any disclosure," tags RETRIEVAL "(inferred)" and PROCESS SHAPE "(inferred, medium confidence)," says "Treat 'world model' as marketing until a paper lands," and volunteers a negative result ("I found NO direct evidence of an adversarial critic pass"). The inference/documentation line is drawn correctly and held.

PUBLISHED RECORD — CONFIRMED against primaries. I fetched both URLs. etnews.com/20260507000224 confirms verbatim: Cobra Agent, MLE-Bench 79.11% vs CAIR MARS+ 62.67 / Baidu FM-Agent 64.44, "환경과 모델의 상호작용을 내부적으로 시뮬레이션하며 미래 결과를 예측하는 월드 모델 방법론" (world-model methodology), and that the paper is only "예정" (planned) — not published. thelec.net/11532 confirms the GIFT-Eval / DeepResearch Bench / DRB-II #1 claims with no scores and no architecture. Searches for a Dalpha/Cobra paper return only unrelated COBRA papers (Mamba VLM, contextual bandits, CHI26 social-agents). HAS PAPER: false is correct.

QUANTITATIVE CLAIMS — reproduced from the artifacts, mostly to the digit:
- Board: 53.10 overall, rank 5, Comp .5258 / Insight .5294 / IF .5387 / Read .5320 — exact; readability IS the board maximum.
- Headings: 3,339 total (exact match), uniqueness 0.895 (claimed 0.896); "Sources" last in 100/100; exec-summary opening 79/100 (claimed 78).
- 83/100 tables, 91/100 horizontal rules — both exact. Median 69 inline [n] markers — exact; density 15.7/1k (claimed 15.1).
- corr(words, insight) = 0.217 (claimed 0.21); corr(words, overall) = 0.262 (claimed 0.27) — exact.
- Median unique URLs: dalpha 30, gemini 106, bodhi 60, openai-DR 41, lunon 11 (claimed 12) — match. Shortest-report ordering holds.
- Conditional routers: I count 47/100 (claimed 48).

COMPOSITION MECHANISM — the load-bearing part, and it is confirmed in unusual detail, not hand-waved. id 44 contains literally every element claimed: a 口径 scope section; a parameter table whose third column is BASIS with citations [7][8]; a LOW/MID/HIGH scenario table yielding 约2.4万 / 3.4万–4.5万 / 4.8万–5.7万 (= the claimed 24k–57k, exact); an exec summary stating "约3万—6万根/年，中位判断约4万根/年左右" (= "30k-60k/yr, median judgment ~40k/yr", exact); the independent tender cross-check "石家庄…860根、合同金额144.48万元，折算约1,680元/根" with an explicit reconciliation of why it disagrees; and §3.1 titled "先说限制：公开份额不等于真实市场份额" with exactly four enumerated reasons, placed BEFORE the share tables, followed by tables labeled 样本A/样本B with sample total 约796.1万元 (= the claimed RMB 7.961M, exact) and a refusal to generalize. id 66 has the scope contract before any candidate, "not documented List view" cells, per-candidate Strengths/Weaknesses/Verdict, and an exec-summary 1-5 ranking IDENTICAL to the Bottom line ~4,000 words later. id 68 has Patterns A-F + router. id 1 has the 9-tier CN framework with 口径说明. Citation indices recur heavily across distant sections in id 44, supporting the "global bibliography, no per-section silos" argument.

DEFECTS FOUND (all peripheral, none touching the architecture):
1. FALSE sub-claim: id 72 "zero web URLs." The report contains 35 http(s) URLs — 34 are https://doi.org/... resolver links and 1 is academic.oup.com. The spirit (journal-only, DOI-based, no blogspam) is right; the literal statement is wrong.
2. NOT REPRODUCIBLE: "median paragraph 37 words." I get 27 (blank-line blocks) or 14 (per-line). Short-paragraph claim holds directionally; the number does not.
3. NOT REPRODUCIBLE: "40/100 evidence-gap statements" and "43/100 labeled assumptions." My best regexes reach 30 and ~25-30. The phenomenon is real and pervasive; the exact counts likely come from an unspecified classifier and should be treated as soft.
4. SOFT: micro-form comparison numbers are systematically inflated by ~20-40% relative to my tokenizer (dalpha median bullet 7 not 9; gemini 39 not 56; cellcog 19 not 23, 48% not 55% bold-led; median words 4,706 not 5,224). Every comparison's DIRECTION and ordering survives; the absolute figures are tokenizer-dependent.
5. INTERPRETIVE OVERREACH (not mislabeled fact, but unearned): "the single cheapest insight+instruction-following lever on the board" and "it wins on the RANKING itself" are causal claims with no ablation or per-feature regression behind them. They should read as hypotheses.

Bottom line: the architecture is inferred, is labeled as inferred, and every inference is anchored to artifact text I independently reproduced. No place crosses the line into stating reverse-engineered design as documented design. Fix the four numeric items above and the claim stands.
input.corrected:
Corrections to fold in (everything else stands as written):

1. id 72 — replace "35 refs is a journal article with a DOI and zero web URLs" with: "all 35 refs are journal articles; the only URLs present are 34 https://doi.org/ resolver links plus one academic.oup.com publisher page — i.e. zero non-scholarly web URLs." (The report does contain 35 http(s) URLs; the claim as written is literally false.)

2. MICRO-FORM — the absolute figures are tokenizer-dependent and ~20-40% high. On my count: dalpha median paragraph 27 words (not 37), median bullet 7 (not 9), 18.7% bold-led (not 20%); gemini median bullet 39 (not 56); cellcog 19 (not 23) and 48% bold-led (not 55%). All comparative orderings hold. Report these as "short paragraphs (~27-37w depending on segmentation)" or re-run with a stated tokenizer.

3. LENGTH — median words dalpha 4,706 / EN 3,754 / CN 5,570 on my counter (claimed 5,224 / 3,956 / 6,290). The "shortest on the board by 2-4x" conclusion is unaffected.

4. FREQUENCY COUNTS — "40/100 evidence-gap" and "43/100 labeled assumptions" are not reproducible by regex (I reach ~30 and ~25-30); either state the classifier used or downgrade to "~30-40/100". "48/100 routers" reproduces (47). "31/100 scope sections" is definition-sensitive (I get up to 49 with a loose pattern).

5. CAUSAL LANGUAGE — "the single cheapest insight+instruction-following lever on the board" and "it wins on the RANKING itself" are untested causal hypotheses (no ablation, no per-feature regression). Mark them as such, consistent with the rest of the claim's labeling discipline.
